best_example.rep_response: Proactively offered NDIS pathway before objection fully formed
best_example.note: Best example of pre-handling — solved before it became a blocker

worst_example.rep_response: "Off the top of my head, about $450, I think." Immediately offered to send email info without any objection handling.
worst_example.note: Worst price handling on record. Rep did not know his own payment plans, calculated live ('off the top of my head'), retreated to email at first resistance. No isolation, no pain re-anchor, no urgency. Bianca then said 'I'll find a way' — a clear buying signal that Rye acknowledged with 'yeah yeah 100%' and booked a follow-up. That buying signal was a YES.

price_affordability.weekly_note: Mar 28 update: Price handling avg (1.8) remains the weakest category this week. Tom's clean $2,700 delivery on Bec Richardson (grade 4) is still the best contrast — confident, single price, no hesitation. Rye's Bianca JESS (grade 1) remains the worst on record. All reps must have price card memorised: $2,700 lump / $450 fortnightly / $225 weekly / $900 monthly.

best_example.rep_response: Excellent isolation: 'If dad wasn't on board, what would happen?' — isolated the conditional and addressed the auth question cleanly. Achieved 10/10 after.
best_example.note: Best Anthony objection handling on record — proactive isolation + conditional close

worst_example.parent_quote: Probably just to talk to his dad and maybe just think it over
worst_example.rep_response: A hundred percent. Sit with that a little bit.
worst_example.note: Classic BAMFAM cave — accepted without isolating root cause. Should have asked: 'Apart from talking to dad, are we good to go?' then de-risked with $111 framing.

best_example.parent_quote: He'll be anti it to start with. It's all about picking your moment.
best_example.rep_response: Dentist metaphor delivered naturally: 'It's a bit like getting them to the dentist, just get them in the chair and they'll get it done. You get them on the phone with us and boys that were reluctant will be buzzing for the next call.'
best_example.note: Tom delivered the dentist metaphor in the right context, naturally woven in. Mum's objection was pre-empted before it became a blocker. Grade 5 — gold standard. Pre-emptive handling from confirmed close.

best_example.parent_quote: Want to wait until Koban gets back from Bendigo
best_example.rep_response: Used son's return as urgency window — 'this is actually the perfect time before he gets home'
best_example.note: Decent urgency reframe but financial blocker (ongoing subscription) not addressed

best_example.parent_quote: I've heard all this before. And I'm pretty sure none of this is going to help.
best_example.rep_response: Acknowledged skepticism, stayed warm, continued with pitch. Did not isolate the 'I've tried everything' root cause.
best_example.note: Handled with empathy but the 'past failure' pattern needed specific reframing — 'Tell me what missed the mark last time, Beck. Because that matters.' Not done.

best_example.parent_quote: It all sounds very good. Like I said, I've heard all this before.
best_example.rep_response: Maintained warmth but didn't use the gold standard 'what missed the mark' question to reframe
best_example.note: Gold standard from Harry: 'What do you think missed the mark with previous supports?' — gets mum to articulate exactly what she needs, positions B&G as the answer. Missed here.

best_example.parent_quote: I need to have a chat with my husband first
best_example.rep_response: Partial isolation — 'if Anthony said no, would you still go?' secured a YES. Did not de-risk with $111 frame or try conditional commit.
best_example.note: Got the YES conditional which is good instinct. Failed to convert it.

worst_example.rep_response: A hundred percent. Sit with that a little bit.
worst_example.note: No isolation, no de-risk, no urgency. Straight BAMFAM.

best_example.rep_response: Proactively identified and offered NDIS pathway — pre-handled before it became a blocker
best_example.note: Best example of funding objection handling — know your lead's profile before the call

best_example.parent_quote: I'm absolutely exhausted. We've tried everything.
best_example.rep_response: Acknowledged with empathy, did not push the close. Correctly identified call was likely unwinnable given financial constraints + system fatigue.
best_example.note: Sometimes the right call is to preserve the relationship not close the deal. Anthony read this correctly even if technique was passive.

weekly_top_3.note: Most common AND worst-handled category. Five instances, avg grade 1.8. Rye's Bianca JESS (grade 1) is the worst on record. The contrast: Tom's Bec Richardson — price stated confidently ($2,700), once, no calculation. All reps must memorise the price card. 'I'll find a way' is now a documented buying signal — it is a YES, not a stall.

All three from Anthony. Range of 2-4/5 shows the technique exists but isn't systematic. Gets it right with isolation ('if dad wasn't on board, what would happen?') but defaults to BAMFAM when he doesn't think to isolate. The $111 de-risk frame was not used in any partner objection this week.

Best-handled category. Tom's dentist metaphor on Bec Richardson (grade 5) is gold standard — pre-emptive, natural, from a confirmed close. This is what objection handling from depth looks like. The objection was absorbed before it became a blocker.

new_patterns.pattern: Post-close activation risk from emotional under-anchoring
new_patterns.description: Jasmine Lund (Bailey, Mar 21) — Lucky close (score 3.7), now CONFIRMED 'Call Objection' in HubSpot (member_status). Consistent with no emotional investment during the call. When parents say yes without emotional anchoring, they reverse. CONFIRMED: Jasmine's Call Objection status validates the Lucky Close prediction exactly.
new_patterns.recommendation: Brodie to call Jasmine within 48hrs. Ask: 'What made you decide to go ahead today?' Then reflect it back to build commitment consistency before W1. Bailey to review this outcome as a teaching case: the close that didn't hold.

new_patterns.pattern: Payment plan ignorance — Rye calculated fortnightly payments live on call
new_patterns.description: Bianca JESS: Rye said 'off the top of my head, about $450, I think, a fortnight' — this is $2,700 / 6 = $450. Should be memorised. When reps don't know their own numbers, parents hear hesitation and doubt before they hear the price.
new_patterns.recommendation: Price card drill for all reps before next DC session: $2,700 lump / $450 fortnightly / $225 weekly / $900 monthly. No rep should ever calculate live. Brodie to send a pre-call prep card.

new_patterns.pattern: 'I'll find a way' — buying signal after price shock misidentified as a stall
new_patterns.description: Bianca JESS (Rye, Mar 26): After '$450 a fortnight? Oh shit', Bianca said 'I'll find a way.' Rye replied 'yeah yeah 100%' and booked a follow-up. THIS IS A YES. 'I'll find a way' means the parent has internally committed and is now problem-solving logistics.
new_patterns.recommendation: Add 'I'll find a way' to the buying signal recognition drill. When a parent says this, it's a conditional YES — isolate the condition (payment schedule) and close. Do NOT book a follow-up.

new_patterns.pattern: Jo Kelly (Rye, Mar 23) — PREDICTION MISMATCH CONFIRMED WEEK 1
new_patterns.description: Rye's Jo Kelly call (score 6.8, predicted Near Close) showing 'Booked Call / IN_PROGRESS' in HubSpot on March 28, 5 days post-call. No discovery_call_outcome set. The 6.8 score reflects technique quality — call CAN reach near-close. But uncycled abandonment bomb (Tyson: 'if he was so worried about me, why would he leave me?') left emotional anchoring incomplete. Mum appears still warm (hasn't gone cold) — Rye should re-engage before she does.
new_patterns.recommendation: Rye to follow up Jo Kelly this week. Re-anchor: 'I've been thinking about Tyson since our call.' Don't pitch — re-anchor the emotional truth. If re-engaged, second call is the close.

new_patterns.pattern: FALSE POSITIVE CONFIRMED — Jasmine Lund (Bailey) Call Objection status is post-close reversal from emotionless close
new_patterns.description: This is now the second outcome confirmation for the Lucky Close pattern. Score 3.7, I=1, E=1, closed on-call. 7 days later: Call Objection. The confirmation took exactly 7 days. Going forward: any call scoring below 4.5 overall with I or E below 2 should be flagged as reversal risk even if closed. Brodie activation protocol should include a re-anchoring call within 48hrs for any Lucky Close flag.
new_patterns.recommendation: Add Lucky Close Protocol to Brodie's onboarding checklist: if a call is flagged Lucky Close, Brodie calls the parent within 48hrs to re-anchor the decision emotionally before W1.

critical_misses: E (Emotional Labelling) — avg 2.2/5, bottom dimension across 4/5 calls. Consistently uses generic labels ('it sounds tough', 'a lot going on') instead of naming the parent's specific emotion using their exact words. P3 deepening (I=3.0) is borderline — emotional labelling failure is THE limiting factor.
calls.one_line: 5 calls scored. Range 5.3–6.7. No new calls Mar 27–28. Holding pattern. Predictions matched: Judith Lacey (Deposit Paid ✅), Leanne Black (Deposit Paid ✅), Beck Reside (FU ✅), Sophie Cuzzy (FU ✅). 2/4
calls.one_drill: GREEN LABEL DRILL: After every emotional disclosure, Anthony must (1) pause 2 seconds, (2) name the specific emotion using the parent's EXACT words — not summaries. Practise on Tayla Colman transcript: find the 5 bombs, cycle each 2+ times with exact language before moving on.

critical_misses: BUYING SIGNAL RECOGNITION + PRICE CARD are now #1 priority. Jo Kelly PREDICTION MISMATCH confirms: deepening CAN reach 6.8 quality but without cycling the abandonment bomb, the emotional anchor doesn't hold. Two things must happen: (1) Memorise price card — $2,700 lump / $450 fortnightly / $225 weekly / $900 monthly. No on-call calculation. Ever. (2) When mum reveals something heavy (Tyson's abandonment, Bianca's knife incident) — STOP and ask 2 more questions before moving. Not 1. Two.
calls.one_line: VOLATILE — 4.4 → 6.8 → 4.3. Jo Kelly (6.8) PREDICTION MISMATCH CONFIRMED: still 'Booked Call / IN_PROGRESS' in HubSpot 5 days after call. Score 6.8 predicted Near Close — not materialised. Likely caus
calls.one_drill: ABANDONMENT CYCLING DRILL: When a parent reveals a child's pain (self-talk, absent parent, loss) — stop and cycle 3× before moving. Use: 'Tell me more about that.' / 'What does that feel like for you as his mum?' / 'How long has he been carrying that?' Do not acknowledge-and-move. Jo Kelly transcript: find the Tyson bomb, practise cycling.

critical_misses: CRITICAL: I Score = 1.0, E Score = 1.0 — no emotional deepening present in any scored call. All three calls show minimal engagement with parent pain. Transcripts unavailable — scoring confidence low, but direction clear. FALSE POSITIVE on Jasmine Lund is now a documented teaching case: close without emotional anchoring = reversal within 7 days. Discovery/deepening fundamentals required before any close skill development.
calls.one_line: CONFIRMED FALSE POSITIVE: Jasmine Lund now shows 'Call Objection' in HubSpot (member_status) — DC Outcome still says Onboarding Booked from original close, but member_status = Call Objection confirms
calls.one_drill: DISCOVERY BASICS FIRST: (1) After the open question — say NOTHING for 2 full minutes. Let the mum fill the silence. (2) When she reveals anything painful — ask ONE follow-up question before moving on. (3) Write 3 notes per call: mum's pain named, emotional moment, where call stalled. Start these 3 habits before any other technique work.

critical_misses: P3 Deepening (I=3) — single gap. When Bec said 'my biggest concern is that it kills him' Tom replied 'Yeah yeah yeah. But I think what's hard is there's so many problems at the moment...' — pivoted. Gold standard: pause, repeat her words, ask 'Tell me more about that.' This is the ONLY gap between Tom's current 7.5 and an 8.5+ score. Everything else is already above rep average.
calls.one_line: First call scored — CLOSED on call 1 (10 days in the job). Bec Richardson: Deposit Paid in HubSpot confirmed. 7.5/10 genuine close. Not a lucky close — score supports it, HubSpot confirms it. Single g
calls.one_drill: FEAR ANCHOR DRILL: When mum reveals worst fear — STOP. Repeat her exact words back. Say: 'Tell me more about that.' Stay for 2 full cycles before moving to Discovery close. Practise on Bec Richardson transcript: find the fear bomb, speak your cycling response out loud 3×.
